title: Skill types, StringOrList deserializer, and frontmatter parsing
purpose: Establish the skill module's type system and file loading infrastructure. All other norn-skills briefs depend on SkillMetadata, StringOrList, and the loader. NC-001 provides the shared frontmatter splitter; this brief builds the skill-specific deserialization on top of it, including the custom StringOrList deserializer that makes Claude Code SKILL.md files parseable.
task: Create the skill/ folder module with types.rs (SkillMetadata with serde rename_all = kebab-case, StringOrList custom deserializer, supporting enums) and loader.rs (frontmatter parsing into SkillMetadata, skill file discovery, lenient validation with diagnostics). The loader uses util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter() from NC-001. StringOrList handles both 'arguments: issue branch' (string) and 'arguments: [issue, branch]' (list) YAML shapes.

requirements.title: Skill module structure
requirements.spec: skill/mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. It SHALL NOT contain functions, structs, traits, or enums.
acceptance: crates/norn/src/skill/mod.rs exists
mod.rs has pub mod types and pub mod loader
mod.rs has pub use re-exports for SkillMetadata, SkillEffort, SkillContext, SkillShell, StringOrList
crates/norn/src/lib.rs declares pub mod skill

requirements.title: StringOrList custom deserializer
requirements.spec: skill/types.rs SHALL define a StringOrList type that deserializes from either a space-separated YAML string or a YAML list of strings. Serialization SHALL produce a list.
acceptance: serde_yaml::from_str('"issue branch"') deserializes to StringOrList(["issue", "branch"])
serde_yaml::from_str('["issue", "branch"]') deserializes to StringOrList(["issue", "branch"])
serde_yaml::from_str('"single"') deserializes to StringOrList(["single"])
serde_yaml::from_str('[]') deserializes to StringOrList([])
StringOrList provides as_slice() and into_vec() accessors

requirements.title: SkillMetadata with full field set and kebab-case serde
requirements.spec: skill/types.rs SHALL define SkillMetadata with #[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")] and all 18 fields from D2: name, description, when_to_use, license, compatibility, metadata, argument_hint, arguments, disable_model_invocation, user_invocable, allowed_tools, model, effort, context, agent, paths, shell, hooks.
acceptance: SkillMetadata has all 18 fields matching the D2 table
YAML with hyphenated keys (disable-model-invocation, user-invocable, allowed-tools, argument-hint, when-to-use) deserializes correctly
disable_model_invocation defaults to false, user_invocable defaults to true
arguments, allowed_tools, and paths use StringOrList type
hooks field is Option<serde_json::Value>
metadata field is Option<HashMap<String, String>>
Unknown fields do not cause deserialization errors

requirements.title: SkillEffort, SkillContext, SkillShell enums
requirements.spec: skill/types.rs SHALL define SkillEffort (Low/Medium/High/XHigh/Max), SkillContext (Fork), and SkillShell (Bash/PowerShell) enums with lowercase serde deserialization.
acceptance: All SkillEffort variants deserialize from lowercase strings
SkillContext::Fork deserializes from 'fork'
SkillShell::Bash and PowerShell deserialize from 'bash' and 'powershell'

requirements.title: Frontmatter parsing with lenient validation
requirements.spec: WHEN a SKILL.md file is loaded, skill/loader.rs SHALL split the content using util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter(), deserialize the YAML into SkillMetadata with deny_unknown_fields NOT set, and apply lenient validation: warn on name mismatch or length, skip on missing description.
acceptance: loader.rs imports and calls util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter()
YAML with all fields deserializes correctly
YAML with unknown fields does not error
A skill with missing description is skipped and a diagnostic is recorded
A skill with name exceeding 64 chars produces a diagnostic warning but is still loaded
A skill with name not matching directory name produces a diagnostic warning but is still loaded

requirements.title: Name defaulting from directory
requirements.spec: WHEN the name field is absent from the SKILL.md frontmatter, the loader SHALL default the name to the directory name (for <name>/SKILL.md) or the file stem (for <name>.md).
acceptance: A SKILL.md in my-skill/ with no name field produces SkillMetadata.name == Some("my-skill")
A flat file deploy.md with no name field produces SkillMetadata.name == Some("deploy")
A SKILL.md with name: custom produces SkillMetadata.name == Some("custom")

requirements.title: Skill file discovery
requirements.spec: WHEN given an ordered list of search directories, the loader SHALL discover skills by checking <name>/SKILL.md and <name>.md patterns in each directory.
acceptance: A directory containing my-skill/SKILL.md is discovered as skill 'my-skill'
A directory containing alias.md is discovered as skill 'alias'
If both my-skill/SKILL.md and my-skill.md exist, the directory-based skill wins
Discovery returns a list of (name, path, metadata, body) tuples

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement template expansion (backtick-bang, dollar-sign, mustache) — that is NS-003
SHALL NOT implement the SkillCatalog — that is NS-002
SHALL NOT modify tools/skill.rs — that is NS-004
SHALL NOT implement effort mapping — that is NS-005
verification: cargo test -p norn -- skill passes all new tests
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
skill/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic
YAML with hyphenated field names (e.g. disable-model-invocation: true) deserializes correctly
YAML with string-form fields (e.g. arguments: 'issue branch') deserializes to Vec
